name: HTTP API Server
description: FastAPI application exposing all Ledger operations as REST endpoints. Provides backend registration (POST /backends), schema registration and query (POST /schemas, GET /schemas/{backend_id}, GET /schemas/{backend_id}/{table}), schema validation (POST /schemas/validate), migration planning and approval (POST /migrations/plan, POST /migrations/{plan_id}/approve), data export (GET /export/{format}), mock data generation (POST /mock/{backend_id}/{table}), annotation query (GET /annotations), and health check (GET /health). Returns JSON responses with appropriate HTTP status codes. Application factory pattern via create_app(config) -> FastAPI. Mounted on uvicorn single-worker, configurable port (default 7701). Lazy schema loading — schemas loaded on demand via Depends(), not at startup — to meet the 3-second startup constraint. Sync route handlers (def, not async def) since all I/O is file-based. Migration plans stored in-memory with UUID IDs and configurable TTL (default 1 hour). The `ledger serve` CLI command starts this server.

types.name: HttpMethod
types.description: HTTP methods used by API routes.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Severity level for validation violations and migration gate results.

types.name: ExportFormat
types.description: Supported export formats, validated via StrEnum path parameter.

types.name: PlanStatus
types.description: Lifecycle status of a migration plan.

types.name: Violation

fields.name: field
fields.description: Dot-path to the offending field, or empty string for global violations.

types.description: A single validation or gate violation with structured context.

types.name: ErrorResponse

fields.name: violations
fields.description: Complete list of all violations — all are returned, not just the first.

types.description: Standard error response body returned for all error HTTP status codes. Contains full list of violations, never just the first.

types.name: HealthResponse

fields.name: status
fields.description: Always 'ok' if the server is running.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port the server is listening on.

types.description: Response body for GET /health. Zero heavy dependencies — no registry load.

types.name: BackendRegistrationRequest

validators.error_message: backend_id must be 3-64 lowercase alphanumeric characters, hyphens, or underscores, starting with a letter.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description of the backend's purpose.

types.description: Request body for POST /backends to register a new backend.

types.name: BackendRegistrationResponse

fields.name: created
fields.description: True if newly created, false if identical re-registration (200).

types.description: Response body for successful backend registration (201 Created).

types.name: SchemaRegistrationRequest

validators.error_message: table_name must be 2-128 chars, lowercase alphanumeric with dots/underscores, starting with a letter.

fields.name: yaml_content
fields.description: Verbatim YAML text of the schema definition. Stored exactly as provided — no normalization, reformatting, or reordering on ingestion.

validators.error_message: yaml_content must be between 1 byte and 1 MiB.
version must be semantic versioning format (e.g. '1.0.0').

types.description: Request body for POST /schemas. The yaml_content field carries the verbatim YAML text of the schema — it is stored as-is, never normalized, reformatted, or reordered.

types.name: SchemaRegistrationResponse

fields.name: created
fields.description: True if newly created (201), false if identical re-registration (200).

types.description: Response body for successful schema registration.

types.name: SchemaDetail

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Data obligation annotations applied to this schema.

types.description: Full schema detail returned by GET /schemas/{backend_id}/{table}.

types.name: ColumnInfo

fields.name: data_type
fields.description: Column data type as declared in YAML.

fields.name: nullable
fields.description: Whether the column allows NULL values.

fields.name: primary_key
fields.description: Whether this column is part of the primary key.

types.description: Parsed column metadata from a schema definition.

types.name: AnnotationEntry

fields.name: propagated
fields.description: True if this annotation was propagated from another schema.

types.description: A single annotation entry with propagation metadata.

types.name: SchemaListResponse
types.description: Response for GET /schemas/{backend_id} listing all schemas for a backend.

types.name: SchemaSummary

fields.name: column_count
fields.description: Number of columns in the schema.

fields.name: annotation_count
fields.description: Number of annotations on this schema.

types.description: Summary of a registered schema without full YAML content.

types.name: SchemaValidationRequest

fields.name: yaml_content
fields.description: Verbatim YAML text of the schema to validate.

validators.error_message: yaml_content must be between 1 byte and 1 MiB.

types.description: Request body for POST /schemas/validate — validates without registering.

types.name: SchemaValidationResponse

fields.name: valid
fields.description: True if schema has zero error-severity violations.

fields.name: violations
fields.description: All violations found, across all severity levels.

types.description: Response body for POST /schemas/validate. Returns all violations, not just the first.

types.name: MigrationPlanRequest

validators.error_message: sql_content must be between 1 byte and 5 MiB.

types.description: Request body for POST /migrations/plan.

types.name: MigrationDiff

fields.name: details
fields.description: Operation-specific detail map (e.g. old_type, new_type for alter_type).

types.description: A single diff entry describing one change in a migration plan.

types.name: GateResult

fields.name: passed
fields.description: True if no error-severity violations — warnings/info do not block.

fields.name: violations
fields.description: Full list of all gate violations across all severity levels.

types.description: Result of migration gate checks. Includes all violations with severity levels.

types.name: MigrationPlanResponse

fields.name: plan_id
fields.description: UUID identifier for the migration plan.

fields.name: gate_result
fields.description: Gate check results including all violations.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this plan expires (default: creation + 1 hour).

types.description: Response body for POST /migrations/plan (201 Created).

types.name: MigrationApproveResponse
types.description: Response body for POST /migrations/{plan_id}/approve.

types.name: ExportResponse

fields.name: schema_count
fields.description: Number of schemas included in the export.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Serialized export content (JSON string, CSV text, or YAML text).

types.description: Metadata wrapper for GET /export/{format}. Actual content returned via appropriate Response subclass (JSONResponse, PlainTextResponse for CSV/YAML).

types.name: MockGenerationRequest

fields.name: row_count
fields.description: Number of mock rows to generate.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Random seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed is used.

types.description: Request body for POST /mock/{backend_id}/{table}.

types.name: MockGenerationResponse

fields.name: table_name
fields.description: Table mock data was generated for.

fields.name: rows
fields.description: List of rows, each a list of values matching columns order.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Seed that was used for generation (for reproducibility).

types.description: Response body for POST /mock/{backend_id}/{table}.

types.name: AnnotationsResponse

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: All annotations across all backends and tables.

types.description: Response body for GET /annotations.

types.name: LedgerConfig

fields.name: schema_dir
fields.description: Directory path where schema YAML files are stored.

fields.name: plan_ttl_seconds
fields.description: TTL for in-memory migration plans in seconds.

validators.error_message: plan_ttl_seconds must be between 60 and 86400 (1 minute to 24 hours).

fields.name: arbiter_url
fields.description: URL for the Arbiter API. Empty string means Arbiter integration is disabled.

types.description: Configuration loaded from ledger.yaml. Lightweight parse at app creation, not at module import.

types.name: InMemoryPlanStore

fields.name: plans
fields.description: Map from plan_id (UUID string) to stored plan data.

types.description: In-memory storage for migration plans. Plans do not survive server restarts. Expired plans are cleaned up lazily on access.

functions.name: create_app
functions.description: Application factory: creates and configures a FastAPI instance with all routers mounted, exception handlers registered, and lazy-loading dependencies configured. This is the sole entry point for constructing the Ledger HTTP server. Config is parsed once (lightweight YAML read of ledger.yaml). No schema data is loaded — lazy loading via Depends() defers that to first request.

error_cases.name: invalid_config
error_cases.condition: config fails Pydantic validation

error_cases.name: yaml_parse_error
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml is malformed (only if config was loaded from file in this call path)

error_data.detail: Clear parse error message with file path

preconditions: config is a valid LedgerConfig instance with all required fields populated
config.schema_dir points to an existing readable directory (not validated at create_app time — validated lazily on first schema access)
postconditions: Returned FastAPI app has all 7 routers mounted: backends, schemas, migrations, export, mock, annotations, health
Exception handlers registered for SchemaValidationError, MigrationGateError, ResourceNotFoundError, ConflictError
No schema data has been loaded into memory — registry is lazy
App state contains config reference and uninitialized registry dependency

structured_side_effects.description: Pure factory function — no I/O, no file reads. Config is passed in pre-loaded.

functions.name: handle_health
functions.description: GET /health handler. Returns server status with zero heavy dependencies — no registry loading, no file I/O. Must respond instantly even if no schemas have been registered.
postconditions: Response status is always 'ok' if the server is running
No schema data loaded as a side effect of this call

structured_side_effects.description: Pure status check — no I/O, no state mutation.

functions.name: handle_register_backend
functions.description: POST /backends handler. Registers a new backend. Returns 201 with Location header on new creation, 200 if identical re-registration, 409 if conflicting re-registration (same backend_id, different display_name or description).

error_cases.name: conflict
error_cases.condition: backend_id already exists with different display_name or description

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic validation

preconditions: body passes Pydantic validation including backend_id regex and display_name length
postconditions: If 201: backend is registered and retrievable; Location header set to /backends/{backend_id}
If 200: no state change (identical re-registration)
If 409: no state change (conflicting registration rejected)

structured_side_effects.description: Adds new backend entry to in-memory registry if not already present.

functions.name: handle_register_schema
functions.description: POST /schemas handler. Registers a schema for a backend. The yaml_content is stored verbatim — never normalized, reformatted, or reordered. Returns 201 with Location header on new creation, 200 if identical re-registration, 409 if version conflict.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Schema registration payload with verbatim YAML content.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: body.backend_id does not reference a registered backend

error_cases.name: conflict
error_cases.condition: Schema with same backend_id, table_name, version already exists with different content

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: yaml_content is not valid YAML

error_cases.name: schema_validation_failed
error_cases.condition: Parsed schema fails structural validation

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic field validation

preconditions: body passes Pydantic validation
body.backend_id references a registered backend
body.yaml_content is valid YAML (parsed to verify, but stored verbatim)
postconditions: If 201: schema is registered, yaml_content stored verbatim, Location header set to /schemas/{backend_id}/{table_name}
If 200: no state change (identical re-registration with same version and yaml_content)
If 409: no state change (same backend_id+table_name+version but different yaml_content)
Annotation propagation is triggered for the newly registered schema
side_effects: Reads schema YAML files from disk on lazy init

structured_side_effects.description: Adds or updates schema entry in the in-memory schema registry.
Lazy initialization may read schema directory on first access.
Triggers annotation propagation rules for the newly registered schema.

functions.name: handle_get_schemas_for_backend
functions.description: GET /schemas/{backend_id} handler. Returns list of all schemas registered under a backend. Triggers lazy loading of registry if not yet initialized.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: backend_id does not reference a registered backend

preconditions: backend_id is a non-empty string
postconditions: Response contains all schemas for the given backend, possibly empty list if backend exists but has no schemas
Schema registry is initialized (lazy load triggered if first access)

structured_side_effects.description: Lazy initialization may read schema directory on first access.

functions.name: handle_get_schema_detail
functions.description: GET /schemas/{backend_id}/{table} handler. Returns full schema detail including verbatim YAML, parsed columns, and annotations.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: backend_id does not reference a registered backend

error_cases.name: schema_not_found
error_cases.condition: No schema registered for this backend_id + table_name combination

preconditions: backend_id and table_name are non-empty strings
postconditions: Response contains full schema detail with verbatim yaml_content as originally registered
Annotations include both direct and propagated entries

structured_side_effects.description: Lazy initialization may read schema directory on first access.

functions.name: handle_validate_schema
functions.description: POST /schemas/validate handler. Validates a schema YAML without registering it. Always returns 200 with validation results including all violations (not just the first). The valid field is true only if there are zero error-severity violations.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Schema validation payload with verbatim YAML content.

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic field validation

preconditions: body passes Pydantic field validation (yaml_content is non-empty, within size limit)
postconditions: Response.valid is true iff zero Violation entries have severity == 'error'
All violations are returned, not just the first
No schema data is persisted — validation only
No state mutation of any kind

structured_side_effects.description: Pure validation — no state mutation, no file I/O.

functions.name: handle_create_migration_plan
functions.description: POST /migrations/plan handler. Parses a SQL migration script, computes diffs against the current registered schema, runs gate checks, and stores the plan in memory with a UUID and TTL. Returns 201 with the plan including all gate violations. Gate failure does NOT produce an error status — it returns 201 with gate_result.passed=false and the full violation list.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Migration plan request with raw SQL.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: backend_id does not reference a registered backend

error_cases.name: schema_not_found
error_cases.condition: No schema registered for backend_id + table_name

error_cases.name: sql_parse_error
error_cases.condition: SQL content cannot be parsed into migration diffs

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic field validation

preconditions: body passes Pydantic field validation
body.backend_id references a registered backend
body.table_name references a registered schema under that backend
postconditions: A new plan with UUID plan_id is stored in InMemoryPlanStore with status 'pending'
Plan expires_at is set to now + config.plan_ttl_seconds
gate_result contains ALL violations with severity levels — never truncated
Expired plans encountered during this call are cleaned up (lazy eviction)

structured_side_effects.description: Stores new migration plan in InMemoryPlanStore with UUID key.
Lazily removes expired plans encountered during access.
May trigger lazy registry init to resolve current schema for diff computation.

functions.name: handle_approve_migration_plan
functions.description: POST /migrations/{plan_id}/approve handler. Approves a pending migration plan. Only plans with status 'pending' and gate_result.passed=true can be approved.

inputs.name: plan_id
inputs.description: UUID of the migration plan to approve, from URL path.

error_cases.name: plan_not_found
error_cases.condition: No plan exists with the given plan_id, or plan has expired

error_cases.name: plan_not_pending
error_cases.condition: Plan status is not 'pending' (already approved or expired)

error_cases.name: gate_failed
error_cases.condition: Plan gate_result.passed is false — cannot approve a plan that failed gate checks

preconditions: plan_id is a valid UUID string
postconditions: Plan status is updated to 'approved' in InMemoryPlanStore
approved_at timestamp is set to current UTC time in ISO 8601

structured_side_effects.description: Updates plan status to 'approved' and sets approved_at timestamp.
Lazily removes expired plans encountered during access.

functions.name: handle_export
functions.description: GET /export/{format} handler. Exports all registered schemas in the specified format. JSON format returns JSONResponse; CSV and YAML formats return PlainTextResponse with appropriate content type.

inputs.name: format
inputs.description: Export format from URL path, validated via StrEnum.

error_cases.name: invalid_format
error_cases.condition: format is not a valid ExportFormat variant

preconditions: format is a valid ExportFormat variant (enforced by StrEnum path validation)
postconditions: Response contains all currently registered schemas serialized in the requested format
JSON format: Content-Type application/json
CSV format: Content-Type text/csv via PlainTextResponse
YAML format: Content-Type text/yaml via PlainTextResponse
No state mutation of any kind

structured_side_effects.description: May trigger lazy registry init to load schemas for export.

functions.name: handle_generate_mock
functions.description: POST /mock/{backend_id}/{table} handler. Generates mock data rows based on a registered schema's column definitions. Uses faker for PII-annotated columns and stdlib random for all others, per project standards.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: backend_id does not reference a registered backend

error_cases.name: schema_not_found
error_cases.condition: No schema registered for backend_id + table_name

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Request body fails Pydantic field validation (row_count out of range)

preconditions: body passes Pydantic validation (row_count 1-10000)
backend_id references a registered backend
table_name references a registered schema under that backend
postconditions: Response contains exactly body.row_count rows
Each row has values matching the schema's column order
PII-annotated columns use faker for realistic mock data
Non-PII columns use stdlib random
If seed is provided, output is deterministically reproducible
No state mutation of any kind

structured_side_effects.description: May trigger lazy registry init to resolve schema for mock generation.

functions.name: handle_get_annotations
functions.description: GET /annotations handler. Returns all data obligation annotations across all backends and tables, including both directly declared and propagated annotations.
postconditions: Response contains all annotations from all registered schemas
Each annotation indicates whether it was propagated or directly declared
total_count matches len(annotations)

structured_side_effects.description: May trigger lazy registry init to resolve annotations.

functions.name: serve_cli
functions.description: Click command handler for `ledger serve`. Loads config from ledger.yaml, creates the FastAPI app via create_app(), and starts uvicorn with workers=1. This is the CLI entry point, not an HTTP handler.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: Port to listen on. CLI option --port, defaults to config value or 7701.

inputs.name: host
inputs.description: Host to bind to. CLI option --host, defaults to '127.0.0.1'.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Path to ledger.yaml. CLI option --config, defaults to 'ledger.yaml'.

error_cases.name: config_not_found
error_cases.condition: config_path does not exist or is not readable

error_data.detail: File path included in error message

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml is malformed YAML

error_data.detail: Clear parse error with file path

error_cases.name: port_in_use
error_cases.condition: The specified port is already bound by another process

preconditions: config_path points to a readable YAML file
port is a valid port number (1-65535)
postconditions: uvicorn server is running on host:port with workers=1
Server blocks until interrupted (Ctrl+C / SIGINT)
side_effects: Starts HTTP server on specified port

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses ledger.yaml configuration file.
Starts uvicorn HTTP server that binds to the specified host and port, accepting inbound connections.
uvicorn logs server startup, shutdown, and request activity.

functions.name: get_registry
functions.description: FastAPI dependency (Depends) that provides the schema registry. Lazy-initialized on first call — does not load schemas until a request actually needs them. Subsequent calls return the cached instance. This is the key mechanism for meeting the 3-second startup constraint.

error_cases.name: schema_dir_not_found
error_cases.condition: config.schema_dir does not exist or is not readable

error_cases.name: schema_dir_unreadable
error_cases.condition: Filesystem permission error reading schema_dir

preconditions: create_app has been called and app state contains config
postconditions: Returns initialized schema registry instance
On first call: registry is created and schema directory is scanned
On subsequent calls: cached registry returned with no additional I/O
side_effects: Reads schema directory on first invocation

structured_side_effects.description: On first invocation, scans schema directory and reads YAML files to populate the registry.
Caches the initialized registry instance in app state for subsequent calls.

invariants: All HTTP error responses use the ErrorResponse model with a complete list of violations — never just the first violation
Schema YAML content is stored verbatim — never normalized, reformatted, or reordered on ingestion or retrieval
POST creation endpoints return 201 with Location header; identical re-registration returns 200; conflicting re-registration returns 409
Schema validation endpoints return ALL violations across all severity levels in a single response
Migration gate failures return 200/201 with gate_result.passed=false — they are not HTTP errors
Migration plans are stored in-memory only and do not survive server restarts
Expired migration plans are cleaned up lazily on access — no background cleanup thread
Health endpoint (GET /health) has zero heavy dependencies and responds without triggering schema loading
Schema registry is lazily initialized on first request, not at app startup, to meet the 3-second startup constraint
All sync route handlers use def (not async def) since all I/O is file-based
Server runs with exactly 1 uvicorn worker
PII-annotated columns in mock generation use faker; all other columns use stdlib random
Missing Arbiter integration: warn and skip canary registration, never crash
Invalid YAML: fail with clear parse error and file path in the violation message
Export: CSV and YAML formats use PlainTextResponse; JSON uses JSONResponse

data_access.rationale: Reads schema YAML files from disk (INTERNAL tier) containing table definitions that may include PII column annotations (e.g. 'pii:email', 'pii:ssn'). Reads PII-tier data only in the sense that schema metadata describes which columns contain PII — the API never reads actual PII values from databases. Writes are INTERNAL-tier only: in-memory registration of backends, schemas, and migration plans. Mock data generation produces synthetic PII via faker but never accesses real PII data.

side_effects.rationale: Reads schema YAML files from the configured schema_dir to populate the lazy-loaded registry. Files contain table structure definitions, column types, and annotation tags.
Mutates in-memory state for backend registration, schema registration, and migration plan storage. All in-memory, no database writes.
Binds to a network port via uvicorn to accept inbound HTTP requests. Single-worker, configurable port (default 7701).
Generates synthetic PII data (emails, names, SSNs, etc.) via faker library for mock data endpoints. This is synthetic data only — no real PII is read or stored.

authority.rationale: The API component is authoritative for: (1) HTTP route definitions, status code contracts, and request/response models for all Ledger REST endpoints; (2) the ErrorResponse/Violation schema used by all error responses across the application; (3) the in-memory migration plan lifecycle including UUID generation, TTL management, lazy eviction, and status transitions (pending -> approved -> expired).
